2604.26243.title: StratMem-Bench: Evaluating Strategic Memory Use in Virtual Character Conversation Beyond Factual Recall
2604.26243.paper_summary: Achieving realistic human-like conversation for virtual characters requires not only a simple memorization and recall of past events, but also the strategic utilization of memory to meet factual needs and social engagement. Current memory utilization relevant (e.g., memory-augmented generation, long-term dialogue, and etc.) benchmarks overlook this nuance, treating memory primarily as a static repository of facts rather than a dynamic resource to be strategically deployed in dialogues. To address this gap, we design StratMem-Bench, a new benchmark to evaluate strategic memory use in character-centric dialogues. This dataset comprises 657 instances where virtual characters must navigate heterogeneous memory pools containing required, supportive, and irrelevant memories. We also propose a framework with different evaluation metrics including Strict Memory Compliance, Memory Integration Quality, Proactive Enrichment Score and Conditional Irrelevance Rate, to evaluate strategic memory use capabilities of virtual characters. Experiments on StratMem-Bench which leverage the state-of-the-art large language models as virtual characters show that all models perform well at distinguishing between required and irrelevant memories, but struggle once supportive memories are introduced into the decision process.
2604.26243.paper_summary_zh: 達成虛擬角色的現實人類對話不僅需要簡單的記憶和回憶過去事件，還需要策略性地利用記憶以滿足事實需求和社交互動。<lb/>當前與記憶利用相關的基準（例如，增強記憶生成、長期對話等）忽略了這一細微差別，主要將記憶視為靜態的事實儲存庫，而非在對話中策略性部署的動態資源。<lb/>為了解決這一問題，我們設計了StratMem-Bench，一個新的基準，用於評估角色中心對話中的策略性記憶使用。<lb/>這個數據集包含657個實例，其中虛擬角色必須在包含所需、支持和無關記憶的異質記憶池中進行導航。<lb/>我們還提出了一個框架，包含不同的評估指標，包括嚴格記憶遵從性、記憶整合質量、主動豐富分數和條件無關率，以評估虛擬角色的策略性記憶使用能力。<lb/>在StratMem-Bench上的實驗利用了最先進的大型語言模型作為虛擬角色，顯示所有模型在區分所需和無關記憶方面表現良好，但在支持性記憶被引入決策過程後則面臨挑戰。
2604.26243.author: Yerong Wu et.al.
2604.26243.authors: Yerong Wu, Tianxing Wu, Minghao Zhu, Hangyu Sha, Haofen Wang
